title: How to obtain a police certificate - El Salvador - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate - El Salvador
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

## Name of the documents you need
- Constancia de Antecedentes Penales (also known as Solvencia de Antecedentes Penales )
- Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales (also known as Solvencia de Antecedentes Policiales )

## Police certificate and IRCC requirements
You must provide a police certificate when you apply for:
- some temporary residence programs
Check your application package or instructions for details.

### If you are a citizen of El Salvador
Apply in person or through an authorized third party for a Constancia de Antecedentes Penales through the Dirección General de Centros Penales in San Salvador (see Contact information for the complete addresses) or apply online .
Apply for a Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales online or in person through the Dirección General de la Policía Nacional Civil y de Readaptación (see Contact Information for the complete address).
Online applications shall be finalized and sent via email within 5 business days, but there may be exceptions.

### If you are not a citizen of El Salvador

#### If you live in El Salvador
Apply for a Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales online or in person through the Dirección General de la Policía Nacional Civil (see Contact Information for the complete address).
Apply for the Antecedentes Penales online or in person or through an authorized third party for the Constancia de Antecedentes Penales at the Dirección General de Centros Penales y de Readaptación in San Salvador (see Contact Information for the complete address).

#### If you live outside of El Salvador
Apply for a Constancia de Antecedentes Penales and Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales in person at your nearest consulate of El Salvador.
Please note: In person services outside of El Salvador to obtain the Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales can take two to three months, as the consulate sends the request to Foreign Affairs of El  Salvador by diplomatic bag, and they will forward it to the police station, where the background is consulted. When the Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales is processed, it will be returned using the same procedure.
El Salvador nationals and residents:
- the original and a photocopy of your valid Documento Único de Identificación (DUI) or your valid passport
- the original and a photocopy of your passport
- two frontal pictures and two pictures of your right side
- the proper completed form (available at the consulate)
- a letter stating why you require a police certificate (i.e., for  immigration purposes)

## Special considerations
The Unit of Police Background Check reserves the right to not issue the Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales to people who were found to have criminal records and/or have not cleared their legal situation.
In the event of an inconvenience obtaining the Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales , you may dial the toll-free number +1 888 301 1130 of the virtual consulate. You may contact your nearest consulate of El Salvador as well.
Dirección General de Centros Penales y de Readaptación (Constancia de Antecedentes Penales)
- Oficina Central 19 Calle Poniente, #320 entre 3a y 5a Avenida Norte Colonia Guadalupe, Edificio Duarte Novoa San Salvador Telephone: 2235-4095
